rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Banned words — grep zero-tolerance.
Ritual / practice (noun) / ceremony — hard ban
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: No ritual/ceremony/practice-noun in body. The AE101 M4 practice-noun earned-landing is owned by the module file's opener, not this exercise; absence here is correct.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: No stale markers, no "fixed" retrospectives, no session biography.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Dated/compounded markers (2026-, rewritten 2026-05-21) all sit below the line-105 maintainer cut, which is exempt.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Register match.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: AE101 quintet honored. Boris-direct/factual framing ("Heavy run expected", "Traces are data"), Risto does NOT lead (M4 deepened-unease mood respected — "readiness without completion", "first experiment of a two-run arc"). No L&D-coach or motivational-poster register. No atmospheric-imagery or negation-callout self-contradiction in body.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Rough-cut drafts for articles.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Articles-only rule; target is a curriculum exercise.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Credit original when paraphrasing named technique.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Huryn credited inline by name for the three-block frame (line 89); gap-analysis named as generic business-analysis framework; Klaassen/compound credited in maintainer block. No creator-name ("Antti") or author-we in body.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Always "you", never "the student"; teacher framing stays out of body.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Body uses second person throughout. "the student" / "trainer forces a slice" appear only below the line-105 maintainer cut (lines 130, 133, 153 etc.), which is exempt.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Don't smuggle terms of art from maintainer into body.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Gap analysis and three-block memory are earned in body via plain description before naming ("walk the system you have against the system the task needs"; quote-from-own-work before naming). No bare self-compound/substrate/three-pattern leak.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Over-hedge detector.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: No reassurance-shaped sentences (no penalty / it's OK if / fine way to). The stop-the-run option is stated structurally ("stop the run when you've seen enough", "Traces are data either way") without apologising for it.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Verbatim-frame-cite in reference artefact.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Reference-artefact authoring rule; not applicable to an exercise body.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Cap practitioner attribution at one student-side mention per module.
rules_evaluated.evidence: 15:"See Huryn's three-block frame in your own material." | 79:"into Huryn's three blocks" | 89:"Huryn's three-block memory." | 93:"into Huryn's three blocks against your own material."
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Huryn named four times in the body of one exercise. Rule treats 3+ as bookkeeping/insecurity. Let the Framework callout at line 89 carry the credit; the What-you-do (15), prompt lead-in (79), and What-happened recap (93) can say "the three-block frame" / "the frame" without re-crediting.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Punchy framing keeps punch; carve-out lives downstream.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Punchy lines ("Every send-off is a test, not a production run") are not over-broad slogans needing a boundary paragraph; the scoping carve-outs (epic vs typo-fix, two-three not all five) already sit one beat downstream.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Value-prop / positioning framing belongs in sales copy + project strategy docs, NOT student-facing curriculum body.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: No defensive/positioning phrases in body. No "vendor" anywhere; "a lesson no lecture can land" is a pedagogical claim about the send-off mechanic, not a marketing-vs-practitioner frame.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Watch out for planting jargon too early.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Terms used (gap analysis, three-block, observations, ADRs, connector, subagent) are at or below M4 arc vocabulary and each lands operationally in this module. No LLM-internal jargon (token, embedding, inference) planted in lead-ins.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Audit closer when trimming body-prose forcing function.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: No dangling demonstratives or bypass-the-agent punchlines. Sections close on example/maxim; the gap-fill forcing function lives inside the fenced AskUserQuestion prompt, with the bullet list as reference after the fence.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Past tense for exercise recap sections — heading and body.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Recap at line 93 uses canonical `**What happened:**` heading with past-tense narration ("You picked... You walked... filled... let Claude rearrange... The frame named... Phase 3 ended"). Stable-state mechanism line (line 58 "The agent reads...") correctly stays present.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: No combative verbs about the agent in body prose.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Human-to-agent verbs stay peer-flat (ask, tell, push back, let Claude propose, screen, walk through). No bark/order/command/whip/drag/boss.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Aspirational destination, never corrective gap.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Frames forward ("first experiment of a two-run arc", "you are testing and you are learning") without corrective-gap or ability-tier pegging. Pre-prompt gates (gitignore check, observations-path note) are plausibly-failable on a real repo.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Earn every technical term on first use.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Bare M1/M4/M5 fine — file is at M4 in the AE101 arc (convention earned by M2). "observations/" and "connector" earned with one-breath primers (lines 54, 58, 68). Huryn frame earned via quote-from-own-work before naming.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Business-audience word ban (Agents 101 default).
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: AE101 file; audience is software-engineer ICs, so the Agents-101 SVP-marketing word ban does not gate this surface. Engineer-appropriate terms (subagent, ADR, connector, idempotency) are in-register and earned.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Memory vs context — use deliberately.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: "three-block memory" / observations used for persistent artifact; "That's still context" (line 69) names runtime-readable knowledge correctly. No "your context compounds" conflation.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Golden path only in body.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Body stays forward-moving. Recovery/what-if branches (sleep freezes session, Ctrl+C corruption, re-run on too-many-items) live below the line-105 maintainer cut, not in body.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Trust Claude's defaults for structure.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Prompts specify shape only where the teaching depends on it (ranked top-5, quote-first/name-second, AskUserQuestion flow, show-me-the-diff). Output destinations named per fill shape.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Q1/Q2/Q3 retro interview is banned at every module. Compound-shape varies per module — never repeat same Debrief.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: No Q1/Q2/Q3 retro. The compound step is owned by the module Debrief (referenced at lines 99-103), described as a one-shot evidence-rewrite + push-back, not a repeated retro interview.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Don't smuggle another module's voice.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Holds M4 readiness-without-completion mood; explicitly resists M5's unleashed-leverage voice ("un-packaged is by design", "teaches Module 5 what packaging adds"). No M1 joyful-creation tone bleed.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Don't ask student to manually diff or spot-check Claude's output as error-catching.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Error-catching is delegated to Claude (audit-as-subagent, "ask Claude to quote different examples"). Push-back lines are emotional/substance reads ("misses something about the codebase", "doesn't match the codebase"), not manual diff for error-catching.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Simulation personas never alone.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: No self-study simulation in this exercise.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: No "What research says" callout boxes
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: No research callout boxes; framework attributions are one-line inline ("Framework: Huryn's three-block memory").

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Student moves stay in conversation with agent.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Forcing function stays in chat with Claude (screen, audit, fill via AskUserQuestion, rearrange, show diff). Connector wiring is a Claude Code action, not an editor detour. The `/rename` is a session command, not an out-of-conversation mechanic.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Specify which CLAUDE.md layer — every time.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Body names layers explicitly: `CLAUDE.md` vs `CLAUDE.local.md` distinguished at line 58, `./CLAUDE.local.md` (personal) with the team PR-against-`CLAUDE.md` carve-out at line 67. No unqualified `CLAUDE.md` smuggling team-governance.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: No em-dashes. Simple sentences.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: No em-dashes in body (lines 1-104). Sentences are short and read-aloud friendly.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Section headers use command verbs when section is about doing something.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Action sections lead with verbs: "Pick the task", "Walk and fill", "Run the audit", "Fill the gaps", "See the frame". "What happened:" is canonical recap, exempt.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Inline links use target's title, never filename.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: No inline cross-surface markdown links in body. The reference path at line 154 (`reference/claude-code-for-engineers.md § 9`) is below the maintainer cut, exempt.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Disambiguate shared-word collisions with Claude Code primitives on first student-authored use.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: First authored write to `observations/` is disambiguated at line 58: named as the student-authored path the agent reads when a prompt names it, distinct from auto-loaded `CLAUDE.md`/`CLAUDE.local.md` and (in prompt) from Claude Code's own `~/.claude/projects/<project>/memory/`.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: LLM-vs-agent-vs-Claude vocabulary discipline.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Body Claude:agent ratio is ~21:5, but the Claude hits are sanctioned addressee ("Ask Claude to...", "tell Claude", "let Claude propose") and product/UI ("Claude Code session", "Claude Code action") forms, while "the agent" correctly marks autonomous-action topics ("hurt the agent most", "the agent reads observations/", "the agent can chew on"). Not the 10:0 assistant-shaped drift the rule targets.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: No time-of-day anchors in body prose.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: No tomorrow/overnight/tonight/morning anchors. Session-relative phrasing used instead ("while you step away", "when you've seen enough", "next module"). Laptop-awake/plugged-in are preserved mechanics, not time-of-day.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Reflection beats stay invisible in flow.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: No pedagogy-aware headers (Meta-moment / Step back / Pause and reflect) and no contemplative-pause sentence prefixes. Reflective prompts ride natural command-verb transitions ("Name which ones you already knew about", "Read the examples first").

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: No meta-ask when student has already decided.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: The AskUserQuestion in walk-and-send-off-3 IS the picking mechanism (student has not yet committed which gaps before the prompt fires), not Claude asking a student to restate an already-written decision. This is the deliberate 2026-05-21 rewrite away from the opt-in speed-up shape.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: `.md` is default file type for student artifacts across curriculum.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Saved artifact is `./task.md` (prompt walk-and-send-off-1). Fills land in `observations/` and `./CLAUDE.local.md`. No `.txt`/`.docx` substitution.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Cut NVA: true but not moving student forward.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Body detail is point-of-use (gitignore-before-writes warning, observations-path note land where the fills happen). Mechanism trivia (sleep-freeze, Ctrl+C corruption, token budget) is kept below the maintainer cut.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Body "read it" / "look at it" / "notice X" after chat-print = dead weight.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Post-chat-print lines carry a follow-on decision, not bare narration: "Read the ranked list. Name which ones you already knew about" (line 48) and "Read the examples first. If they're from your own files, the frame should click" (line 85). Each points at the next move.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Forward-callout closer: student as grammatical subject.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Forward callouts keep "you" as subject ("You'll use it again next module", "You'll see next module what the others were for", "You'll use it forever for every agent hand-off"). No training-as-subject, no coach-imperative, no role-gating (no "if you came in as a CTO").

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Operational reminders live at moment of use, not only where introduced.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Module-open operational state is restated at the top of the exercise: "Start clean" branch/working-tree reminder (line 13), `/rename` session command (lines 9-11), and the laptop-awake/plugged-in send-off mechanic at the close.
